none.justification: The answer gets the exact total of 6 and five of the six call sites/functions right, but misidentifies the enclosing function for kernel/exit.c:1310 instead of naming wait_task_stopped(), which is part of the required enumeration. Its mm_update_next_owner acquire/release evidence at 508 -> 521/527 is otherwise a valid GT-approved pairing.

ccodegraph.justification: It lists all 6 required call sites with the correct enclosing functions, including the two mm_update_next_owner sites and excluding put_task_struct_rcu_user. The wait_task_zombie pairing is fully evidenced with get_task_struct(p) at 1112 and put_task_struct(p) at 1117 on the WNOWAIT path.

codegraph.justification: It correctly enumerates all 6 call sites with functions and gives a GT-approved mm_update_next_owner pairing from get_task_struct(c) at 508 to the mutually exclusive releases at 521 and 527. The explanation demonstrates the branch structure rather than merely asserting balance.

cbm.justification: It correctly lists the exact 6 sites and enclosing functions, and it excludes the distinct put_task_struct_rcu_user symbol. Its mm_update_next_owner trace cites get_task_struct(c) at 508 and the mutually exclusive releases at 521/527 with sufficient control-flow evidence.
